label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. Jane was born on the last day of February in 2001. Today is her 16th birthday. I need to find the date one year ago from today in MM/DD/YYYY format. The options are given, so maybe I can work through the problem step by step. First, let's figure out Jane's birthdate. The last day of February is usually the 28th, except in a leap year when it's the 29th. But 2001 is not a leap year because leap years are divisible by 4. 2000 was a leap year, then 2004, 2008, etc. So 2001's February had 28 days. Therefore, Jane was born on 02/28/2001. Today is her 16th birthday, so today's date would be the same day of the year, but 16 years later. So adding 16 years to 2001 gives 2017. Therefore, today's date is 02/28/2017 because that's the 16th birthday. Now, the question is asking for the date one year ago from today. If today is 02/28/2017, then one year ago would be 02/28/2016. But wait, I need to check if 2016 is a leap year. 2016 is divisible by 4 (2016 ÷ 4 = 504), so yes, it is a leap year. However, even though 2016 is a leap year, the date one year before 02/28/2017 is still 02/28/2016 because February 28 exists in both leap and non-leap years. The leap day is February 29, which doesn't affect the 28th. So the date one year ago would still be February 28, 2016. Looking at the options, option C is 02/28/2016. Let me check the other options to make sure I didn't make a mistake. Option A is 02/28/2015, which would be two years before her 16th birthday (since 2017-2=2015). That's not correct. Option B is March 31, 2016, which doesn't seem related. Option D is way in the past (1992). Option E and F are March dates that don't make sense in this context. So the answer should be option C.
